Assembly: The assembly fDipBim1.hap2.1 is based on 25x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A heterozygous chromosomal fission was observed on chromosome 1.Complex heterozygous reciprocal translocations and associated rearrangements were observed between chromosomes 10 and 12.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP1 chromosome 12 (69,200 - 72,900 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP2 chromosome 12 are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on HAP1 chromosome 13 (79,700 - 83,700 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly ilCerLeuc1.1 is based on 38x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
